full_response:
Step-1.  Write down the game in matrix form  
(row player = Droberts, column player = first-mate; first coordinate = mate’s payoff, second = Droberts’ payoff):

                              First-mate  
                       Mutiny        No-mutiny  
Droberts Greedy   (3 , 0)        (0 , 3)  
          Generous  (1 , 2)        (2 , 1)

Let  
 p = Pr[ Droberts plays Greedy ]  (so 1–p = Pr[ Generous ])  
 q = Pr[ first-mate mutinies ]     (so 1–q = Pr[ No-mutiny ]).

In a mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium every pure strategy that is played with positive probability must give the player the same expected payoff; otherwise the player would deviate to the better one.

------------------------------------------------------------------
Step-2.  First-mate’s best-response condition (compute payoffs as a function of p)

If the mate Mutinies (M):    E[payoff] = 3·p + 1·(1–p) = 1 + 2p.  
If the mate stays loyal (N): E[payoff] = 0·p + 2·(1–p) = 2 – 2p.

Indifference (needed if 0<q<1):  
     1 + 2p = 2 – 2p  ⇒  4p = 1  ⇒  p = ¼.

Check incentives:  
 Δ = (M payoff) – (N payoff) = (1+2p) – (2–2p) = 4p – 1.  
 When p < ¼  Δ < 0 ⇒ mate prefers No-mutiny.  
 When p > ¼  Δ > 0 ⇒ mate prefers Mutiny.  
Thus at p = ¼ the mate is happy to mix with any q.

------------------------------------------------------------------
Step-3.  Droberts’ best-response condition (compute payoffs as a function of q)

If Droberts is Greedy (G):   E[payoff] = 0·q + 3·(1–q) = 3 – 3q.  
If Droberts is Generous (R): E[payoff] = 2·q + 1·(1–q) = 1 + q.

Indifference (needed if 0<p<1):  
     3 – 3q = 1 + q  ⇒  4q = 2  ⇒  q = ½.

Check incentives:  
 Δ = (G payoff) – (R payoff) = (3–3q) – (1+q) = 2 – 4q.  
 When q < ½  Δ > 0 ⇒ Droberts prefers Greedy.  
 When q > ½  Δ < 0 ⇒ Droberts prefers Generous.  
Thus at q = ½ Droberts is willing to mix with any p.

------------------------------------------------------------------
Step-4.  Intersection of the two indifference conditions

First-mate indifferent  ⇒  p = ¼.  
Droberts indifferent    ⇒  q = ½.

Therefore the unique mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium is

 Droberts: Greedy with probability p = ¼, Generous with probability ¾.  
 First-mate: Mutiny with probability q = ½, No-mutiny with probability ½.

At these probabilities both players obtain the same expected payoff from their two pure options, so no unilateral deviation is profitable.

\boxed{p = \tfrac14,\; q = \tfrac12}

Confidence: 0.88
